IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly inspect trees growing near overhead power lines in your area. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close to power lines. If left unmanaged, branches may come into contact with the overhead lines, which could lead to unexpected interruptions to your electricity supply. To help prevent this, we need to trim back the trees that are growing near the power lines. As power lines cannot be worked on with live electricity flowing through them, a temporary power cut is required to ensure the safety of both our staff and the public. This essential work will involve tree cutting across 2 sections of the overhead electricity network. Doing this helps reduce the risk of you experiencing unexpected power cuts in the future and maintains a safe and reliable electricity supply as we appreciate that some customers may have experienced multiple unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work is taking place near Mill Way.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly inspect, maintain and upgrade the electrical equipment in your area. As part of this programme, we will be upgrading 5 electricity poles. These poles play an important role in supporting the overhead electricity network and helping us deliver a safe and reliable supply to homes and businesses. This work will also reduce the likelihood of unexpected power cuts in the future as we appreciate that some customers may have experienced an unplanned power cut in the last 6 months. To help reduce the time the power is off, our contractors IPSUM may carry out some preparation work before the planned power cut. You may see their vans in your area, but they won't need access to your property. We understand that power interruptions are inconvenient and we will do everything we can to keep disruption to a minimum. This work is taking place off Wells Road.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely trim trees away from the overhead cables. This work will cover approximately 75 metres across 1 section of the network. Due to the extent of the work and the safety checks required before and after the trimming, a longer shutdown is required due to volume of work needing to be carried out. Completing this work in one visit helps reduce the risk of future unplanned outages as we appreciate you may have been impacted by an unplanned power cut in the last 6 months. This work is taking place near Great Canfield Road.

IncidentCategoryCustomerFriendlyDescript: To help keep your electricity supply safe and reliable, we regularly check trees growing near our overhead power lines. During a recent inspection, we found some trees that have grown too close and could interfere with the cables, especially during bad weather. To prevent potential damage and reduce the risk of unplanned power cuts, we will be carrying out essential tree trimming work in your area. Our engineers need to temporarily switch off your power so we can safely trim trees away from the overhead cables. This work will cover across 5 sections of the network. Completing this work in one visit will help reduce the risk of future unplanned outages and keeps your electricity supply safe and reliable as we appreciate your area has been impacted with unplanned power cuts in the last 6 months. This work is taking place near Kirkgate.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Donny at 15:22 - We can now confirm that all power in the Heron Road should be back on. The issue was due to an underground cable fault, however engineers have managed to restore power from the substation nearby and will be investigating the root cause.

If there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the unexpected outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Donny at 14:57 - Thanks for your patience over today.

Our engineers have let us know that they are in the process of pinpointing the faulty section of cabling, then excavations and repairs will be carried out. To give them time to do this safely, the estimated time frame to restore your power is now between 18:30 and 19:30.

We're sorry this is taking longer than expected, and we'll continue to keep you up to date, but if any change to this time comes in from our staff I'll let you know as soon as we hear.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Our team of specialist engineers have arrived on site to locate and fix the underground cable fault.

We'll continue to keep you updated with all of our progress as it comes in from site. Sent by Donny at 14:48.

Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Theo at 10:23 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the Sandy Lane area, the estimated time of 13:00-14:00 hasn't changed. We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them. We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Our staff are trying to find the root cause and fix it permanently, however with this kind of issue it's likely that power will go off multiple times before we're able to find and fix it. For now we have restored your power as quickly as we can.

If your power isn't on or you would like to talk to us, call us for free on 08003163105. We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

I appreciate your continued patience this morning. I know having power cuts as often as this must be very disruptive, and our staff are doing everything they can to get this resolved.

They are still testing our network in the area, and depending on the local infrastructure this can take some time. However, once they update us we'll know if our estimated time frame needs to change and why, and I'll let you know as soon as we hear.

In the meantime, if we can offer any support or advice, our team are always available to talk on 105.

Sent by Theo at 09:22.

Our staff have now arrived on site in the Bell Lane area. Due to there being multiple power cuts over a short space of time, our engineers are going to start their tests at the nearby substation.

Our current time frame is between 12:30 and 13:30; this is an estimate based on them checking the state of our network, and attempting to restore your power.

It's possible that the underground cabling fault we believe has been causing these power cuts has reach a point where they can't safely replace our substation equipment. If this happens we'll need to arrange further teams and equipment, so I'll let you know more news as soon as our staff update us. Sent by Theo at 09:11.

Logs.Comment:
Sent by Theo at 12:25 - Thanks for your patience today.

Our engineers have let us know that repairs to the low overhead cable are in progress. To give them time to do this safely, the estimated time frame to restore your power is now between 14:00 and 15:00.

We're sorry this is taking longer than expected, and we'll continue to keep you up to date, but if any change to this time comes in from our staff I'll let you know as soon as we hear.

Sent by Theo at 11:35 - Hi - thanks for bearing with us, I know a power cut can be very disruptive. We've had an update from our staff that there are now 6 properties still affected by the fault, as they have managed to safely divert some power, getting the lights back on for 575 of our customers.

We'll keep sending text updates to everyone until all the power to your area is on. To stop receiving texts, just follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe.

We know the time frame we initially estimated is now approaching. Our engineers have arrived on site to investigate the reports of a low overhead cable.

As our staff are working with live electricity, we need to wait until it is safe for them to stop and contact us with an update.

We're sorry you've been impacted by this, and we'll provide you an update as soon as possible. Sent by Theo at 11:17.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Theo at 11:32 - We can now confirm that all power in the Tonbridge Road TN11 0 area should be back on. The issue was caused by an unexpected fault with some of our network equipment, which our engineers have now replaced.

We know that being without power can be especially difficult, so we're sorry for the outage. Please rest assured our staff will continue to work around the clock to keep the lights on.

If your power isn't on or there's anything you'd like to talk to us about, call us on 08003163105 which is free from landlines and mobiles.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
Sent by Decasta at 11:08 - Thanks for bearing with us today.

Our engineers are checking our substation equipment to ensure our network is healthy before they attempt to restore your power. They may also walk our cabling route throughout the area to check for any visible problems, check smaller electrical sites in the area, or apply specialist equipment which may restore power temporarily to help them with their testing.

We know that our customers may be worried about the food in the fridge and freezer, so we want to try and help with advice. Food should keep for up to 4 hours in the fridge, and between 24-48 hours in the freezer if you avoid opening them.

We'll keep you updated on our engineers progress.

Steps.Message:
Sent by Decasta at 12:05 - Our engineers have restored power to all 1878 properties in the Ashford TN24/25  area, and we're pleased that this is sooner than we estimated. They have diverted power around an overhead cabling fault.

We know some properties in the area may have faced other recent interruptions, and we're sorry for the extra disruption. Every outage is fully investigated by our engineers, though power may be lost several times before we can safely find and fix the issue. Our customer services team will keep as many customers updated as possible during these investigations. 

We're sorry for the unplanned disruption. If you need to talk to us, or let us know that your power isn't restored, call us on 08003163105.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: Our engineers have arrived on site in the Ashford TN24/25 area and are investigating the overhead network fault. You may find your power is going on and off; that is normal for this type of issue and is due to our engineers working to divert power remotely. By doing this, we've managed to restore power to 1878 customers. There are now 120 properties still affected and using an average time frame we're estimating all power will be on between 14:30-15:30, but this may change later depending on what our staff find. We'll keep sending SMS updates until everybody is restored. To stop receiving texts, just follow this link https://UKPN.short.gy/STOP to unsubscribe. Sent by Decasta at 11:52.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message:
What will happen next?

This outage affects multiple areas, so locations we mention may be further away than you expect. We're arranging staff to attend ASAP, but at the same time we try to restore power remotely by redirecting electricity. It's possible your power may go on and off as we try to get as many customers back on as we can.

Once available, our engineer will head to the local substation and after checking the network, they will attempt to restore more power from there. If unsuccessful, they will work to locate the issue and make repairs, which may take longer to get the lights on.

Our staff work as quickly as they can, so you might not hear about everything we're doing but we'll always give you the clearest information we have.
Steps.Name: Customer Service Team

Steps.Message: We became aware of this power cut at 11:23. We’re sorry for any disruption this may have caused you. We didn’t know in advance there would be a power cut, but now that we know, our teams will do everything they can to get your power back quickly. Engineers in our control room have switched some customers power back on in your area. To get everyone’s power back on we may need to attend to investigate and make repairs. We’ll continue to update you until everyone’s power is back on.
